summary: Refactor: consolidate skill surface, clean deprecated terminology, move runtime code out of scripts, align graph schema to JGF-compatible v2, and add graph-backed state machine with transient build lifecycle.

accepted_decisions.summary: Expose one public codewiki skill. Compiler docs become internal assets under skills/codewiki/references/. Current public focused skills collapse into one router plus internal assets.
Use documentation compiler as the product/process name. plan is deprecated naming. The artifact remains documentation_build.
Use validation gateway as the product term. verifier may remain only as an internal runner role name where implementation needs it.

Move runtime code out of scripts/. codewiki-gateway.mjs and codewiki-transaction.mjs move to extensions/codewiki/src/ or are deleted if obsolete. smoke-test.mjs splits into tests/smoke/*. token-benchmark.mjs may stay as a convenience helper.
Keep JSON as the primary generated graph read model. Evolve toward a JGF-compatible shape with CodeWiki semantic metadata. SQLite is only an optional rebuildable cache, not the primary source.

Builds are transient handoff payloads, not durable history. Lifecycle: proposed -> accepted -> applied -> validated -> archived/soft-deleted -> purged after TTL. Git/session history preserve audit; active graph indexes live/recent builds only.

Adopt a graph-backed state machine. Documents remain canonical truth for intended product/system behavior; roadmap tracks implementation delta below knowledge; tests/code are executable reality; graph is rebuildable state tracker over alignment edges.

Keep three compilers: feedback, documentation, implementation. Add a reconciliation gateway/controller, not a fourth compiler. It reads graph alignment state, decides which loop to enter, and routes drift to feedback/documentation/implementation as appropriate.

Use flexible typed-layer reconciliation rather than a strict feedback->product->system->roadmap->tests->code pipeline. User intent enters through feedback; code/test changes can trigger upward reconciliation through graph drift classification.

assumptions: Pi discovers public skills from package paths and skill directories containing SKILL.md; moving detailed guidance into assets should reduce public prompt noise.
Existing roadmap tasks and tests may still contain deprecated words like plan and verifier and should be cleaned during documentation work.
scripts/codewiki-gateway.mjs and scripts/codewiki-transaction.mjs need inspection before moving or deleting.
open_questions: Should research, architecture, and view-audit remain accessible as internal assets only, or should any stay public despite the one-skill target? Current accepted direction favors one public skill.
non_goals: Do not change the compiler model itself.
Do not add SQLite as a required dependency.
Do not hide canonical knowledge inside generated graph output.

knowledge: Update CodeWiki docs to describe one public skill plus internal assets.
Update terminology from plan/verifier to documentation compiler/validation gateway.
Define graph schema v2 as JSON/JGF-compatible with CodeWiki semantic metadata.
Update runtime-gateway docs after scripts migration decision.
Document build lifecycle and TTL/soft-delete policy.
Document graph-backed state machine and reconciliation gateway/controller.
Clarify roadmap scope: implementation delta below knowledge, not documentation edit queue.
Clarify upward reconciliation: code/test changes can route back to documentation or feedback when graph detects drift.
roadmap: Create task: consolidate public skill surface.
Create task: move tests/runtime code out of scripts.
Create task: define graph schema v2.
Create task: implement/repair feedback loop tooling so accepted feedback builds are real artifacts, not chat-only intent.
Adjust implementation tasks so roadmap represents executable implementation delta, not pure documentation edits.
Add implementation work for build lifecycle management and graph state-machine fields.
Add implementation work for reconciliation gateway/controller if not covered by existing graph/audit tasks.
code: Feedback/documentation/implementation build schemas need lifecycle state and TTL metadata.
Graph needs alignment/state fields and drift classification edges.
State/status tool should surface reconciliation next action from graph state.
